steps.title: Identify the pattern of deliberate choice
steps.content: Both texts emphasize that Impressionists made intentional decisions about frames. Text 1: Degas “insisted” on specific frames; Impressionists “chose” colorful frames to distinguish themselves. Text 2: Artists acted “for aesthetic reasons”; frame colors were deliberately “chosen” to harmonize with paintings.

steps.title: Match intentionality to the answer
steps.content: Choice C directly states what both texts demonstrate: painters were intentional in selecting frames. The other choices either reverse the frame-painting relationship (B), claim unsupported preferences (A), or misstate Degas's relationship to Impressionist practice (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
